# Command reference

Every `gskill` command and its flags, generated from the CLI grammar.

## Global flags

These apply to every command.

| Flag | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--config` | string | Path to a config file. |
| `--dir` | string | Run as if gskill started in this directory. |
| `--dry-run` | bool | Report actions without applying them. |
| `--json` | bool | Emit machine-readable JSON on stdout. |
| `--no-cache` | bool | Bypass the content cache. |
| `--no-interactive` | bool | Disable prompts and colored output. |
| `--offline` | bool | Operate without network access. |
| `--quiet` | bool | Suppress diagnostics on stderr. |
| `--verbose` | bool | Enable verbose diagnostics. |
| `--yes` | bool | Assume yes for confirmation prompts. |


## `add`

Add and install a new skill.

| Argument | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `source` | yes | Skill source: git shorthand, URL, or local path. |

| Flag | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--agent` | []string | Target agent ID (repeatable). |
| `--commit` | string | Explicit commit SHA to pin. |
| `--copy` | bool | Copy instead of symlinking. |
| `--exact` | bool | Pin to the exact resolved version. |
| `--force` | bool | Overwrite an existing declaration and re-resolve. |
| `--global` | bool | Install into the user-global location. |
| `--project` | bool | Install into the project (default). |
| `--ref` | string | Branch or tag to track. |
| `--symlink` | bool | Symlink instead of copying (default). |
| `--version` | string | Semver constraint (e.g. ^2.0.0). |


## `cache`

Manage the content cache.


## `cache clean`

Remove all cached material.


## `cache list`

List cached entries.


## `cache path`

Print the cache directory.


## `cache stats`

Show cache size and entry count.


## `check`

Report fast drift status.

| Flag | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--fail-on-drift` | bool | Exit non-zero (7) if any drift is detected. |


## `completion`

Print a shell completion script.

| Argument | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `shell` | yes | Shell: bash, zsh, or fish. |


## `config`

Inspect layered configuration.


## `config get`

Print one configuration value.

| Argument | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `key` | yes | Configuration key. |


## `config list`

Print the effective configuration.


## `config path`

Print the config file path.


## `diff`

Show manifest/lock/disk differences.


## `doctor`

Check the environment and declared requirements.


## `info`

Show details for one skill.

| Argument | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | yes | Skill name. |


## `init`

Scaffold a gskill project (manifest, state dir, gitignore).


## `install`

Install all declared skills (additive, idempotent).

| Flag | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--copy` | bool | Copy instead of symlinking. |
| `--frozen-lockfile` | bool | Restore exactly from the lockfile; never modify it. |
| `--global` | bool | Install into the user-global location. |
| `--update-lockfile` | bool | Allow the lockfile to be rewritten. |


## `list`

List installed skills and their status.


## `lock`

Recompute the lockfile from the manifest.


## `outdated`

Show skills with newer versions available.

| Flag | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--exit-code` | bool | Exit 8 if any update is available. |


## `remove`

Uninstall skills and clean up.

| Argument | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `skills` | yes | Skills to remove. |


## `repair`

Re-materialize broken installs and clean up staging.


## `sync`

Make disk exactly match the lockfile (--prune removes orphans).

| Flag | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--prune` | bool | Remove agent skill directories not in the lockfile. |


## `tui`

Launch the interactive dashboard.


## `update`

Advance skills within their version constraints.

| Argument | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `skills` | no | Skills to update (default: all declared). |


## `verify`

Re-hash installed content against the lockfile.


## `version`

Print the gskill version.
